response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins which are not present in underwater environments but the presence of water does not eliminate the risk of exposure to peanuts in food or other substances that might be consumed underwater  
Sec. 2  
the physical act of living underwater requires breathing through gills or other means which is not possible for humans and the environment itself does not contain the allergen but the main issue remains the allergic reaction which is unrelated to the aquatic setting but rather to the presence of the allergen in the diet or surroundings
